inputs.name: note
inputs.description: The content/text of the note to be created

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: The type/category of the note (defaults to 'general')

inputs.name: project_id
inputs.description: UUID of the parent project (if note is associated with a project)

inputs.name: task_id
inputs.description: UUID of the parent task (if note is associated with a task)

inputs.name: customer_id
inputs.description: UUID of the parent customer (if note is associated with a customer)

inputs.name: limit
inputs.description: Maximum number of notes to return in pagination (default 50, max 200)

inputs.name: offset
inputs.description: Number of notes to skip for pagination (default 0)

inputs.name: organization_id
inputs.description: Organization UUID extracted from JWT claims for multi-tenancy scoping

outputs.name: note
outputs.description: A note object with id, content, type, timestamps, creator, and parent association
outputs.assertion: should return object with id (UUID), note (string), type (string), project_id/task_id/customer_id (UUID or null), created_at (ISO timestamp), created_by (UUID), updated_at (ISO timestamp), organization_id (UUID)

outputs.name: notes
outputs.description: Array of note objects for a given parent entity
outputs.assertion: should return array of note objects sorted by created_at DESC with pagination metadata (total, limit, offset, has_more)

outputs.name: error
outputs.description: Structured error response with code and message
outputs.assertion: should return object with error_code (string), message (string), and optional details (object)

outputs.name: created_note
outputs.description: Newly created note returned after POST operation
outputs.assertion: should return note object with auto-generated id, timestamps, and created_by matching authenticated user

outputs.name: updated_note
outputs.description: Updated note returned after PATCH operation
outputs.assertion: should return note object with modified note/type fields and updated_at timestamp newer than created_at

outputs.name: deleted_note_id
outputs.assertion: should return the id of the deleted note and subsequent GET should return 404

scenarios.name: Create project note successfully
scenarios.description: User creates a new note associated with a project they have access to
steps: Authenticate user and extract organization_id from JWT
Submit POST /api/projects/{project_id}/notes with note content and type
Backend validates project exists and belongs to user's organization
Backend creates note with project_id, organization_id, created_by from JWT
Backend returns newly created note object
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Note is created with valid UUID, timestamps, and project association; returned object includes all fields and matches input content

scenarios.name: Create task note successfully
scenarios.description: User creates a new note associated with a task they have access to
steps: Authenticate user and extract organization_id from JWT
Submit POST /api/tasks/{task_id}/notes with note content and type
Backend validates task exists and belongs to user's organization
Backend creates note with task_id, organization_id, created_by from JWT
Backend returns newly created note object
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Note is created with valid UUID, timestamps, and task association; returned object includes all fields and matches input content

scenarios.name: Fetch notes for project with pagination
scenarios.description: User retrieves paginated list of notes for a specific project
steps: Authenticate user and extract organization_id from JWT
Submit GET /api/projects/{project_id}/notes?limit=50&offset=0
Backend validates project exists and belongs to user's organization
Backend fetches notes filtered by project_id and organization_id, ordered by created_at DESC
Backend returns paginated response with notes array and metadata
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Returns array of up to 50 notes sorted newest first, with pagination metadata (total, limit, offset, has_more)

scenarios.name: Reject note creation without parent entity
scenarios.description: Backend enforces exactly one parent foreign key constraint
steps: Attempt to create note with no project_id, task_id, or customer_id
Backend validation fails check constraint
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Returns 400 error with code INVALID_REQUEST and message indicating exactly one parent entity is required

scenarios.name: Reject note creation with multiple parent entities
scenarios.description: Backend enforces exactly one parent foreign key constraint
steps: Attempt to create note with both project_id and task_id populated
Backend validation fails check constraint
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Returns 400 error with code INVALID_REQUEST and message indicating exactly one parent entity is allowed

scenarios.name: Enforce organization scoping via RLS
scenarios.description: User cannot access notes from another organization
steps: Authenticate user A from organization X
Attempt to fetch notes for project belonging to organization Y
RLS policy filters notes by organization_id from JWT
Backend returns empty array (no permission error exposed)
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Returns empty array or 404 error; user cannot see notes from other organizations

scenarios.name: Validate note content length
scenarios.description: Backend enforces minimum and maximum content length
steps: Submit note with empty content string
Backend validation fails
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Returns 400 error with code INVALID_NOTE_CONTENT and message indicating content cannot be empty

scenarios.name: Update note content successfully
scenarios.description: User updates the content of an existing note they created
steps: Authenticate user and fetch existing note created by same user
Submit PATCH /api/notes/{note_id} with new note content
Backend validates note exists and belongs to user's organization
Backend updates note field and updated_at timestamp
Backend returns updated note object
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Note content is updated, updated_at is newer than created_at, created_at and created_by remain unchanged

scenarios.name: Delete note successfully
scenarios.description: User deletes a note they have permission to delete
steps: Authenticate user and fetch existing note from their organization
Submit DELETE /api/notes/{note_id}
Backend validates note exists and belongs to user's organization
Backend performs hard delete
Backend returns deleted note ID
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Note is permanently deleted from database; subsequent GET returns 404

scenarios.name: Cascade delete notes when parent project is deleted
scenarios.description: Deleting a project automatically deletes all associated notes
steps: Create project with multiple associated notes
Delete parent project
Database ON DELETE CASCADE triggers
All notes with matching project_id are deleted
scenarios.expectedOutcome: All notes associated with deleted project are removed; no orphaned notes remain

scenarios.name: Reject unauthorized note access
scenarios.description: User cannot access notes without valid authentication
steps: Submit GET /api/notes without authentication header
Backend authentication middleware rejects request
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Returns 401 error with code UNAUTHORIZED

scenarios.name: Handle pagination edge case: offset beyond total
scenarios.description: Request with offset greater than total notes returns empty array
steps: Project has 10 total notes
Submit GET /api/projects/{id}/notes?limit=50&offset=20
Backend calculates has_more as false
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Returns empty notes array with has_more=false and total=10

scenarios.name: Enforce pagination limit maximum
scenarios.description: Backend caps limit parameter at 200 to prevent resource exhaustion
steps: Submit GET /api/notes?limit=500
Backend enforces max limit of 200
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Returns up to 200 notes despite requesting 500; response metadata shows limit=200

scenarios.name: Preserve creator and timestamp during migration
scenarios.description: Migrated notes maintain original created_at and created_by values
steps: Export FileMaker note with ~CreationTimestamp and ~CreatedBy
Map FileMaker creator to Supabase user UUID
Bulk insert note with explicit created_at and created_by
Verify migrated note has original timestamp and creator
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Migrated note has created_at matching FileMaker ~CreationTimestamp and created_by matching mapped user UUID

scenarios.name: Validate parent entity exists before note creation
scenarios.description: Backend rejects note creation if parent entity does not exist
steps: Submit POST /api/projects/{non_existent_uuid}/notes
Backend validates project_id foreign key
Foreign key constraint fails
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Returns 404 error with code ENTITY_NOT_FOUND and message indicating parent project does not exist

scenarios.name: Handle concurrent note creation on same parent
scenarios.description: Multiple users can create notes on the same project simultaneously
steps: User A and User B both authenticated to same organization
Both submit POST /api/projects/{id}/notes concurrently
Backend processes both requests independently
Both notes are created with unique IDs and timestamps
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Both notes are created successfully with different created_by values; no race conditions or conflicts

scenarios.name: Reject note update if user lacks permission
scenarios.description: RLS policy prevents users from updating notes in other organizations
steps: User A from organization X creates note
User B from organization Y attempts PATCH /api/notes/{note_id}
RLS policy filters by organization_id from JWT
Update fails silently or returns 404
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Returns 404 error; user cannot update notes from other organizations

scenarios.name: Return notes sorted by created_at DESC
scenarios.description: Notes are always returned in reverse chronological order
steps: Create 3 notes with timestamps T1, T2, T3 (T1 < T2 < T3)
Submit GET /api/projects/{id}/notes
Backend orders by created_at DESC
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Notes array is ordered [T3, T2, T1] with newest first

scenarios.name: Validate type field defaults to 'general'
scenarios.description: Omitting type field in create request defaults to 'general'
steps: Submit POST /api/projects/{id}/notes with note content but no type field
Backend applies default value 'general' to type column
Backend returns created note
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Created note has type='general'

scenarios.name: Verify organization_id derivation from parent entity
scenarios.description: Note inherits organization_id from parent project/task/customer
steps: Fetch project with organization_id = ORG_X
Create note for that project
Backend derives organization_id from parent project
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Created note has organization_id matching parent project's organization_id
